buildings.name: Abbey Road Studios
buildings.description: The iconic London studio where The Beatles recorded most of their albums, cementing it as the most famous recording studio in the world.

buildings.name: The Troubadour
buildings.description: The legendary West Hollywood club that launched the careers of Elton John, James Taylor, and Joni Mitchell during the 1960s and 70s.

buildings.name: Electric Lady Studios
buildings.description: Jimi Hendrix's custom-built Greenwich Village studio, designed to feel like an underwater paradise and still producing hits today.

buildings.name: FAME Studios
buildings.description: The Muscle Shoals, Alabama studio where Aretha Franklin, Etta James, and Wilson Pickett recorded some of the greatest soul music ever made.

buildings.name: Sun Studio
buildings.description: The Memphis birthplace of rock and roll where Elvis Presley, Johnny Cash, and Jerry Lee Lewis made their first recordings.

buildings.name: CBGB
buildings.description: The grimy Bowery club that gave birth to American punk rock, hosting early performances by The Ramones, Blondie, and Talking Heads.

buildings.name: The Cavern Club
buildings.description: The underground Liverpool venue where The Beatles played nearly 300 shows before achieving worldwide fame.

buildings.name: Motown Hitsville U.S.A.
buildings.description: Berry Gordy's converted Detroit house where The Supremes, Stevie Wonder, and Marvin Gaye defined the sound of a generation.

buildings.name: Chess Records Studio
buildings.description: The Chicago studio that shaped the sound of electric blues and early rock through artists like Muddy Waters, Chuck Berry, and Howlin' Wolf.

buildings.name: The Fillmore
buildings.description: Bill Graham's San Francisco concert hall that became the epicenter of the psychedelic rock movement in the 1960s.

buildings.name: Hansa Studios
buildings.description: The Berlin studio by the Wall where David Bowie recorded his iconic Berlin trilogy and U2 crafted Achtung Baby.

buildings.name: The Apollo Theater
buildings.description: Harlem's world-famous stage whose Amateur Night launched James Brown, Ella Fitzgerald, and countless other Black artists to stardom.

buildings.name: Sunset Sound
buildings.description: The Hollywood studio where The Doors, Led Zeppelin, and Van Halen each recorded career-defining albums.

buildings.name: The Ryman Auditorium
buildings.description: Nashville's Mother Church of Country Music and original home of the Grand Ole Opry, revered for its perfect acoustics.

buildings.name: Compass Point Studios
buildings.description: The legendary Nassau, Bahamas studio founded by Chris Blackwell where AC/DC, Iron Maiden, and Grace Jones recorded landmark albums.

buildings.name: The 100 Club
buildings.description: The Oxford Street venue that hosted the first international punk festival in 1976, featuring the Sex Pistols and The Clash.

buildings.name: RCA Studio B
buildings.description: Nashville's oldest surviving recording studio where Elvis Presley recorded over 200 songs and the Nashville Sound was born.

buildings.name: The Whisky a Go Go
buildings.description: The Sunset Strip club that launched The Doors as its house band and became a proving ground for hard rock and metal acts.

buildings.name: Trident Studios
buildings.description: The Soho studio where Bowie recorded Ziggy Stardust, Queen laid down Bohemian Rhapsody, and the first Bechstein piano tracks were cut.

buildings.name: Red Rocks Amphitheatre
buildings.description: The natural sandstone amphitheatre near Denver whose unmatched acoustics and dramatic setting have hosted legendary live recordings.

buildings.name: J&M Recording Studio
buildings.description: Cosimo Matassa's tiny New Orleans studio where Little Richard, Fats Domino, and Ray Charles forged the blueprint for rock and R&B.

buildings.name: The Bluebird Cafe
buildings.description: The intimate Nashville listening room where Garth Brooks was discovered and songwriters share works-in-progress with rapt audiences.

buildings.name: Criteria Studios
buildings.description: The Miami studio where Eric Clapton recorded Layla, the Bee Gees cut Saturday Night Fever, and Fleetwood Mac tracked Rumours sessions.

buildings.name: First Avenue
buildings.description: The legendary Minneapolis nightclub where Prince filmed Purple Rain and which remains a vital launching pad for indie artists.

buildings.name: Musician's Union Hall
buildings.description: A hub where session musicians gather, ready to join any project that needs them.

buildings.name: Open Audition Stage
buildings.description: An open call stage where any talented musician can walk in and audition on the spot.

buildings.name: Guitar & Bass Workshop
buildings.description: An intensive workshop that trains string players for your label's roster.

buildings.name: Talent Agency
buildings.description: A well-connected talent agency that can swap musicians on your roster for better fits.
